name: Piston Honda mk III
description: Dual wavetable oscillator with 512-wave 8×8×8 morphing cube. Per-oscillator thru-zero FM, unison detune, waveshaper input mode, 8-slot preset manager. Shared X/Y/Z sliders with per-osc select and lock.

controls.name: Frequency (large)
controls.description: Primary tune knob per oscillator. Default is coarse tune; swappable with the small knob in the Global menu. In External In mode this knob becomes the waveshaper Gain control.
controls.detail: coarse (default) or fine · doubles as Ext In Gain

controls.name: Frequency (small)
controls.description: Secondary tune knob. Default fine tune; roles reversible via Freq Knob Mode.

controls.name: CV attenuverter (Freq/Gain)
controls.description: Bipolar attenuverter for the oscillator CV input. Center = no modulation; CCW inverts, CW adds. In Ext In mode controls VCA amount on the external gain.

controls.name: FM/Ext In attenuator
controls.description: Attenuator for the audio-rate thru-zero FM / external-input jack. Keep fully CCW if FM is not desired — normalled from the other oscillator.
controls.detail: thru-zero FM depth · Ext In gain in waveshaper mode

controls.name: Mode button
controls.description: Toggles between Oscillator mode (internal wavetable VCO) and External In mode (wavetable waveshaper processing the FM/Ext In signal).
controls.detail: Osc ↔ Ext · display shows 'Ext' in Ext mode

controls.name: Select button
controls.description: Selects which oscillator receives motion from the shared X/Y/Z sliders and attenuverters. Both may be selected; at least one must be selected. Pressing locks the sliders to prevent accidental cross-editing.
controls.detail: routes X/Y/Z · auto-locks on press/preset change

controls.name: Link button
controls.description: Links Osc B's coarse frequency to Osc A. Fine tune and CV remain active on B. Enables one-touch 2× or 4× unison fat sounds via the Mix output.
controls.detail: B follows A · B's Fine/CV still active

controls.name: X slider
controls.description: Manual position on the X axis of the 8×8×8 wavetable cube. Moves across 8 waves within the current Y/Z plane.

controls.name: Y slider
controls.description: Manual position on the Y axis. Scans a new plane of X-axis waveforms.

controls.name: Z slider
controls.description: Manual position on the Z axis — selects between the 8 loaded wavetable banks (each a user-loadable .wav file on the SD card).
controls.detail: 8 banks · morphs between (disable for hard transitions)

controls.name: X/Y/Z attenuverter
controls.description: One attenuverter per axis CV input. Center = no CV; CCW/CW adds or subtracts from the slider position.

controls.name: Preset / Edit button
controls.description: Enables the 8-slot preset manager. Second press enters Morph mode (smooth CV-driven interpolation). Click rotary encoder while preset is active to enter edit-save state.

controls.name: Preset CV attenuator
controls.description: Attenuates or offsets the Preset CV input. Role depends on CTL option (CV+Offset, CV+Atten, Trig+Offset, Trig Random).

controls.name: Rotary encoder
controls.description: Menu navigation and preset selection. Hold + button combos reach the three main menus (Osc A, Osc B, Global, Preset). Hold + Select to change Octave; hold + Mode to change Unison.

inputs.name: 1V/Oct (Osc A, Osc B)
inputs.description: Tracked pitch input per oscillator. 0V at C0 convention — input voltage maps directly to displayed note name.

inputs.name: CV (Osc A, Osc B)
inputs.description: Bipolar frequency CV per oscillator, scaled by the attenuverter. In Ext In mode controls waveshaper gain.

inputs.name: FM / Ext In (Osc A, Osc B)
inputs.description: AC-coupled audio-rate input. In Oscillator mode provides thru-zero FM. In External In mode provides the signal to be waveshaped through the selected wavetable. Normalized from the other oscillator when unpatched.

inputs.name: Sync (Osc A, Osc B)
inputs.description: Hard-sync input per oscillator. Rising edge resets oscillator phase.

inputs.name: X CV
inputs.description: CV input for X-axis waveform position. Shared between oscillators (can be disabled per-osc via Waveform CV menu).

inputs.name: Y CV
inputs.description: CV input for Y-axis waveform position. Shared/toggleable.

inputs.name: Z CV
inputs.description: CV input for Z-axis (bank) selection. Shared/toggleable.

inputs.name: Preset CV
inputs.description: Scans or triggers the 8 preset slots. Behavior set via CTL (CV+Offset, CV+Atten, Trig+Offset, Trig Random).

outputs.name: A
outputs.description: Main audio output of oscillator A.

outputs.name: B
outputs.description: Main audio output of oscillator B.

outputs.name: Mix
outputs.description: Sum of oscillators A and B. Useful for one-cable unison/link patches.

behaviors.name: 8×8×8 wavetable cube
behaviors.description: 512 16-bit waveforms arranged as a 3D cube. X/Y scan 64 waveforms within a Z bank; Z selects one of 8 SD-loaded .wav banks. Smooth morphing on all axes by default. Per-axis morph disable turns smooth interpolation into hard wave transitions, useful for glitchy wavetable scanning or for hearing stored waves un-mixed.

behaviors.name: Shared X/Y/Z with Select lock
behaviors.description: Sliders and X/Y/Z attenuverters are shared — Select buttons determine which oscillator(s) receive slider motion. After pressing Select or loading a preset, sliders lock until moved. Unlocking per-oscillator prevents editing the wrong voice by accident. Deselecting both is prevented.

behaviors.name: Thru-zero FM
behaviors.trigger: audio signal patched to FM / Ext In · Mode: Oscillator
behaviors.description: Each FM input implements thru-zero FM, keeping pitch stable around zero hertz during deep modulation. Normalled from the other oscillator — attenuator fully CCW disables unintentional self-FM. True through-zero means classic DX-style bell and metallic tones without detuning.

behaviors.name: External Input (waveshaper) mode
behaviors.trigger: Mode button on osc · Ext appears on display
behaviors.description: Large Frequency knob becomes manual Gain; CV attenuverter becomes VCA for gain. Internal oscillator silences; instead the signal at FM/Ext In is dynamically waveshaped through the selected wavetable — classic digital waveshaper sound. Feed a sine for the cleanest harmonic sculpting. Both oscs in Ext mode with nothing patched causes unpleasant feedback.

behaviors.name: Link follows A
behaviors.description: Osc B coarse tune disables; B copies A's pitch. B's Fine Tune and CV still apply — use Fine for unison detune. Combined with per-oscillator Unison, you get up to 4-oscillator stacked sounds through the Mix output with one pitch CV.

behaviors.name: Unison detune
behaviors.trigger: Oscillator menu → Unison · or hold encoder + Mode button
behaviors.description: Doubles the oscillator with a detuned copy summed internally. Detune amount or ±octave settings give thick 2-voice sounds per oscillator. Stacking on both oscs plus Link mode gives a huge 4-voice unison without extra cables.

behaviors.name: Morph enable per axis
behaviors.description: Turn off morph on X, Y, or Z to produce hard wave switching instead of smooth interpolation. Z-off is particularly useful for hearing a single loaded wavetable file without neighbor-bank bleed. Display shows missing colon on a given axis label when morph is disabled there.

behaviors.name: Tone quantization menu
behaviors.description: Introduces subtle quantization distortion for 'classic' digital character reminiscent of prior Piston Hondas. Orthodox is full 16-bit clean. Other modes fold high-frequency aliases back into the audible range — most audible at low frequencies on sine-like waves.

behaviors.name: Octave shift hotkey
behaviors.description: Shifts oscillator pitch across ±2 octaves. Works without menu. Pitch falls silent if you exceed the ~14 Hz minimum.

behaviors.name: Preset morph
behaviors.description: Morph mode locks all managed controls. Encoder selects the base preset (fixes non-morphable menu settings like Unison/Mode). Preset CV interpolates smoothly across 8 stored sound-design states. Preset Scope chooses between Waves Only (stable pitch) and All Params (full morph including pitch).

behaviors.name: microSD wavetable loading
behaviors.trigger: 8 files named 1.wav–8.wav on SD · Global menu → Load Waves from SD
behaviors.description: All 8 Z-axis banks load at once. Each file: 1-channel 16-bit WAV, 32 KB, 64 waveforms × 256 samples. WaveEdit by Synthesis Technology is the recommended authoring tool. Factory wave set still downloadable from industrialmusicelectronics.com for re-filling missing banks.

behaviors.name: Sync hard-reset
behaviors.description: Classic hard sync. Phase resets to zero on each trigger. Combined with X/Y/Z CV, produces sync-scream timbres that also scan wavetables — a sonic region unique to this module.

patchIdeas.name: Wavetable lead with Link unison
patchIdeas.patch: Osc B Link ON · Osc B Fine Tune slightly off center (~10 cents) · X slider 3 · Y slider 4 · Z slider 2 · slow LFO → Y CV (depth ~30%) · Mix output → filter → VCA · detuned dual-osc lead with slowly morphing wavetable position across the Y plane
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* Osc B: Link ON · Fine: +10 cents
* X: 3 · Y: 4 · Z: 2

Sequencer:
LFO:
Filter:
VCA:
Envelope:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Piston Honda mk III (1V/Oct Osc A)
- LFO (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (Y CV)
- Piston Honda mk III (Mix) -> Filter (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Thru-zero FM bell
patchIdeas.patch: Osc A as carrier · Osc B Link OFF · Osc B output normalled to Osc A FM (no cable) · Osc A FM attenuator ~1 o'clock · Osc B Frequency set ~ratio 1:2.76 for bell · X 2 Y 1 Z 1 (sine-like carrier) · envelope → Osc A CV (modulating FM index via attenuverter) · DX-style bell voice from the internal normal
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* Osc A: carrier · FM attn: ~1 o'clock
* Osc B: Link OFF · Freq ratio ~2.76
* X: 2 · Y: 1 · Z: 1

Sequencer:
Envelope:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Piston Honda mk III (1V/Oct Osc A)
- Envelope (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (CV Osc A) // FM index swell
- Piston Honda mk III (A) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Waveshaper on external sine
patchIdeas.patch: External sine VCO → Osc A FM/Ext In · Osc A Mode: Ext In · large Osc A knob = manual gain · envelope → Osc A CV (VCA gain) · X slider scans harmonic complexity per note · classic FM waveshaping voice using the wavetable cube as nonlinear shaper
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* Osc A: Mode Ext In
* X: scan 1-8 · Y: 3 · Z: 1

VCO:
* Wave: Sine

Envelope:

- VCO (Sine) -> Piston Honda mk III (FM/Ext In Osc A)
- Envelope (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (CV Osc A) // VCA gain
- Piston Honda mk III (A) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Hard-sync wavetable scream
patchIdeas.patch: Osc A: master pitch · Osc B: Sync input patched from Osc A (use a mult or patch Osc A out to Osc B Sync) · Osc B Frequency 1 octave above A · sweep LFO → Y CV (both oscs selected) · Osc B Select only for knob edits · classic sync-sweep plus moving wavetable = aggressive lead
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* Osc A: base pitch
* Osc B: one octave up, Link OFF

Sequencer:
Mult:
LFO:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Piston Honda mk III (1V/Oct Osc A)
- Piston Honda mk III (A) -> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out) t> Piston Honda mk III (Sync Osc B)
- LFO (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (Y CV)
- Piston Honda mk III (B) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo dual-osc patch
patchIdeas.patch: Osc A pitched at unison with Osc B but separate 1V/oct inputs for chord · X/Y/Z different per osc (select one at a time to tweak independently) · Osc A → stereo L · Osc B → stereo R · Waveform CV disabled on Osc B (menu) so X/Y/Z only affects A · Z CV (slow LFO) scans banks on A only — asymmetric stereo morph
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* Osc A: selected · X 4 Y 2 Z 1
* Osc B: deselected, Waveform CV Off · X 1 Y 5 Z 3

Chord Seq A:
Chord Seq B:
LFO:

- Chord Seq A (Pitch) p> Piston Honda mk III (1V/Oct Osc A)
- Chord Seq B (Pitch) p> Piston Honda mk III (1V/Oct Osc B)
- LFO (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (Z CV)
- Piston Honda mk III (A) -> Output (L)
- Piston Honda mk III (B) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Preset morph performance
patchIdeas.patch: 8 presets authored with increasing harmonic density · Preset Scope: Waves Only (pitch stays musical) · CTL: CV+Offset · slow sequencer ramp → Preset CV · Morph mode ON · each note transitions voice character across 8 snapshots · stable pitch, fluid timbre — Star Wars organ territory
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* 8 presets: low → high density
* Preset Scope: Waves Only · CTL: CV+Offset · Morph ON

Sequencer:
Ramp:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Piston Honda mk III (1V/Oct Osc A)
- Ramp (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (Preset CV)
- Piston Honda mk III (Mix) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Glitchy wavetable drum
patchIdeas.patch: Osc A: percussive envelope on CV · Morph Enable: Z off (hard bank transitions) · stepped random → Z CV · each trigger lands on a different wavetable bank · Fast X/Y slow modulation · Unison: +OCT for thickness · produces an ever-mutating digital drum voice
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* Osc A: Morph Enable Z off · Unison +OCT
* X: 2 · Y: 3

Trigger:
Envelope:
S&H:
Noise:

- Trigger (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Trigger (Out) t> S&H (Trig)
- Noise (Out) >> S&H (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (CV Osc A)
- S&H (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (Z CV) // each hit = new bank
- Piston Honda mk III (A) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Cross-modulated pair
patchIdeas.patch: Osc A out → Osc B FM In (attenuator ~11 o'clock) · Osc B Mix/A out patched to Osc A FM In (via a mult) · small attenuation both ways · two-way FM creates complex quasi-stable timbres · X/Y/Z mild modulation · pitch tracked on Osc A only, Osc B at ratio by ear
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* Osc A: Mode Osc · FM attn: 11 o'clock
* Osc B: Mode Osc, Link OFF · FM attn: 11 o'clock

Sequencer:
Mult A:
Mult B:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Piston Honda mk III (1V/Oct Osc A)
- Piston Honda mk III (A) -> Mult A (In)
- Mult A (Out) -> Piston Honda mk III (FM/Ext In Osc B)
- Piston Honda mk III (B) -> Mult B (In)
- Mult B (Out) -> Piston Honda mk III (FM/Ext In Osc A) // two-way FM
- Piston Honda mk III (Mix) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Trig-Random preset on accent
patchIdeas.patch: CTL: Trig Random · accent trigger → Preset CV · preset randomizes on every accented note · Preset Scope: Waves Only · pitch stays stable, voice character changes unpredictably on accents · use Reload Presets to return to known states between performances
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* CTL: Trig Random · Preset Scope: Waves Only

Sequencer:
Accent Seq:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Piston Honda mk III (1V/Oct Osc A)
- Accent Seq (Gate) t> Piston Honda mk III (Preset CV) // randomize on accent
- Piston Honda mk III (Mix) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual waveshaper stereo
patchIdeas.patch: Osc A Mode: Ext · Osc B Mode: Ext · different external sources patched to each FM In (breaks the self-feedback warning) · Select buttons flip between A and B independently so X/Y/Z can be dialed per-osc before locking · Osc A → L · Osc B → R · stereo waveshaping rig with independent wavetable processing per side
patchIdeas.diagram:
Piston Honda mk III:
* Osc A: Mode Ext · X/Y/Z set while only A selected
* Osc B: Mode Ext · X/Y/Z set while only B selected

VCO L:
VCO R:
Envelope:

- VCO L (Out) -> Piston Honda mk III (FM/Ext In Osc A)
- VCO R (Out) -> Piston Honda mk III (FM/Ext In Osc B)
- Envelope (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (CV Osc A)
- Envelope (Out) >> Piston Honda mk III (CV Osc B)
- Piston Honda mk III (A) -> Output (L)
- Piston Honda mk III (B) -> Output (R)
